name: Varenyky
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine the flour and salt, forming a well in the center.
2. Add the butter and milk, mixing lightly until the flour is absorbed. If necessary, add a little warm water to achieve the desired consistency.
3. Knead the dough until it sticks together, cover, and allow to rest for a few minutes. Then knead again until smooth. Cover and set aside. (The dough should be soft as additional flour will be added when rolled out.)
- Alternatively, combine the flour, salt, and butter in a food processor, stirring occasionally. Add liquids slowly while the machine is running until a ball forms. Allow to rest for a few minutes before processing until smooth. Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, turn, and cover for about 30 minutes. It may be wrapped in plastic and refrigerated for up to a day or so, then rolled out.
4. On a floured work surface, roll one quarter of the dough into a circle, starting at the center and rolling outward to maintain an even thickness. Turn over and again from the center, roll the dough until it is about 1/8-inch thick.
5. With a 3-inch biscuit cutter or wine glass, cut rounds. Place 1 tablespoon of filling on one side of each round, flip the other half over the top, and seal with fingers or a fork. Ensure proper sealing to prevent the filling from leaking during cooking.
6. Place each dumpling on a floured cookie sheet, keeping them covered with a towel. Repeat with the remaining dough, saving scraps for last. Use minimal flour in this process to avoid toughness.
7. Gently drop 12 to 15 varenyky into a large pot or Dutch oven of boiling water (3 quarts). Do not overcrowd. Stir and cook for about 3 to 4 minutes until they float to the surface.
8. Drain in a colander and place on a lightly oiled cookie sheet, shaking to coat with a thin film to prevent sticking. Do not pile dumplings as this distorts their shape.
9. To freeze, place varenyky on an oiled cookie sheet when still warm (lukewarm) and allow to freeze solid. Store in tightly sealed plastic bags.
Serving Suggestions:
Varenyky may be poached, pan-fried, or steamed. Serve as an accompaniment with meats or with sour cream, chopped sauteed onions, or fried bacon with a little bacon fat.
